---
title: "Fraud_Report_Model_Development"
output: pdf_document
date: "2026-02-22"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r setup install packages}
##Install necessary libraries from this code chunk if needed.
#Skip if packages already installed.

install.packages("dplyr")
install.packages("MASS")
install.packages("lubridate")
install.packages("forecast")
install.packages("here")
```

```{r setup}
#Libraries necessary for code usage.
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(MASS)
library(forecast)
library(here)
```

```{r functions for repetitive tasks}
#------------------------------------------------#
# Inject synthetic anomalies (5% of data)        #
# nc = not contaminated, c = contaminated        #
#------------------------------------------------#

inject_anomalies <- function(data_nc, k = 3) {
  
  data_c <- data_nc
  
  # number of spikes (5% of series)
  n_spikes <- round(length(data_nc) * 0.05)
  
  # standard deviation of series
  s <- sd(data_nc, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # randomly choose points to contaminate
  idx <- sample(seq_along(data_nc), n_spikes)
  
  # inject spikes
  data_c[idx] <- data_c[idx] + k * s   # add k * sd
  
  return(list(
    contaminated_series = data_c,
    spike_indices       = idx
  ))
}

#------------------------------------------------#
#    Convert forecasts to table                  #
#------------------------------------------------#

forecast_table <- function(fc,
                           fraud_type,
                           metric,
                           start_date = as.Date("2025-09-29")) {
  
  future_dates <- seq(
    from       = start_date + 1,
    by         = "day",
    length.out = length(fc$mean)
  )
  
# Convert ts objects to numeric vectors
  lower <- if (!is.null(fc$lower)) {
    matrix(as.numeric(fc$lower), ncol = ncol(fc$lower))
  } else {
    matrix(NA, nrow = length(fc$mean), ncol = 2)
  }
  
  upper <- if (!is.null(fc$upper)) {
    matrix(as.numeric(fc$upper), ncol = ncol(fc$upper))
  } else {
    matrix(NA, nrow = length(fc$mean), ncol = 2)
  }
  
  # Ensure 2 columns for 80% and 95% intervals
  if (ncol(lower) == 1) lower <- cbind(lower, NA)
  if (ncol(upper) == 1) upper <- cbind(upper, NA)
  
  data.frame(
    Date        = future_dates,
    Metric      = metric,
    Fraud_Type  = fraud_type,
    Forecast    = as.numeric(fc$mean),
    Lower_80    = lower[, 1],
    Upper_80    = upper[, 1],
    Lower_95    = lower[, 2],
    Upper_95    = upper[, 2]
  )
}

#---------------------------------------------------------------#
#    Rolling Window Cross Validation (fixed ARIMA structure)    #
#---------------------------------------------------------------#
rolling_validate_arima <- function(series,
                                   fitted_model,
                                   window_size = 30,
                                   h = 1) {
  
  n    <- length(series)
  pred <- rep(NA, n)
  
  for (t in window_size:(n - h)) {
    
    # rolling training window
    train_window <- series[(t - window_size + 1):t]
    
    # refit same ARIMA structure
    fit_roll <- Arima(train_window, model = fitted_model)
    
    # h-step ahead forecast
    pred[t + h] <- forecast(fit_roll, h = h)$mean[h]
  }
  
  # align actual vs forecast
  actual        <- series[(window_size + h):n]
  forecast_vals <- pred[(window_size + h):n]
  
  # error metrics
  mae  <- mean(abs(actual - forecast_vals), na.rm = TRUE)
  mse  <- mean((actual - forecast_vals)^2, na.rm = TRUE)
  rmse <- sqrt(mse)
  
  return(list(
    actual   = actual,
    forecast = forecast_vals,
    pred_all = pred,
    MAE      = mae,
    MSE      = mse,
    RMSE     = rmse
  ))
}
```


```{r importing data}
fraud_data <- read.csv(here("data", "processed", "aggregated_fraud_daily.csv"))
fraud_data$Date.Received...Date.recue <- as.Date(fraud_data$Date.Received...Date.recue, format = "%Y-%m-%d")
```


```{r Checking for historic past data spikes to handle}
#----------------------------------------------#
#   Aggregate all fraud data daily             #
#----------------------------------------------#

fraud_all_daily <- fraud_data %>%
  mutate(Date.Received...Date.recue = 
           as.Date(Date.Received...Date.recue)) %>%
  group_by(Date.Received...Date.recue) %>%
  summarise(total_victims = sum(total_victims, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(Date.Received...Date.recue)

df_area <- fraud_data %>%
  mutate(Date.Received...Date.recue = 
           as.Date(Date.Received...Date.recue)) %>%
  group_by(Date.Received...Date.recue, Fraud_type) %>%
summarise(total = sum(total_victims), .groups = "drop")

## -- Fraud prevention month markers (March 3 each year)  -- ##
years <- unique(format(fraud_data$Date.Received...Date.recue, "%Y"))
march3_dates <- as.Date(paste0(years, "-03-03"))

#----------------------------------------------#
#       Plot: All fraud cases (daily)          #
#----------------------------------------------#
plot(fraud_all_daily$Date.Received...Date.recue,
     fraud_all_daily$total_victims,
     type = "l",
     xlab = "Date",
     ylab = "Total Daily Fraud Victims",
     main = "All Fraud Cases Combined (Daily)")

abline(v = as.numeric(as.Date("2022-10-01")), col = "red", lwd = 2)
abline(v = march3_dates, col = "purple", lty = 2, lwd = 2)

legend("topright",
       legend = c("End of COVID-19 Lockdown",
                  "Fraud Prevention Month"),
       col = c("red", "blue"),
       lty = c(1, 2),
       lwd = 2,
       bty = "n")
lines(smooth.spline(
    as.numeric(fraud_all_daily$Date.Received...Date.recue),
    fraud_all_daily$total_victims
  ), col = "blue", lwd = 2)
mtext(paste("Fig 1", ".","All Fraud Reported Victims"), #captions
      side = 1, line = 4, cex = 0.8)
 
# Note: data does not stabilize until late 2023

#----------------------------------------------#
#      Top 10 fraud types (entire dataset)     #
#----------------------------------------------#
all_top10 <- head(sort(table(fraud_data$Fraud_type), decreasing = TRUE), 10)
all_top10_names <- names(all_top10)

fraud_topall <- fraud_data %>%
  filter(Fraud_type %in% all_top10_names)

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

#----------------------------------------------#
#   Aggregate by fraud type and date           #
#----------------------------------------------#
fraud_daily <- aggregate(
  cbind(total_victims, total_loss) ~ Fraud_type + Date.Received...Date.recue,
  data = fraud_topall,
  FUN = sum
)

all_list <- split(fraud_daily, fraud_daily$Fraud_type)

## -- Plot: Top 10 fraud types over time  -- ##
for (ft in names(all_list)) {
  
  df_ft <- all_list[[ft]]
  df_ft <- df_ft[order(df_ft$Date.Received...Date.recue), ]
  
  plot(df_ft$Date.Received...Date.recue,
       df_ft$total_victims,
       type = "l",
       main = ft,
       xlab = "Date",
       ylab = "Daily Victims")
  
  abline(v = as.numeric(as.Date("2022-10-01")), col = "red", lwd = 2)
  abline(v = march3_dates, col = "purple", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
  
  legend("topright",
         legend = c("End of COVID-19 Lockdown",
                    "Fraud Prevention Month"),
         col = c("red", "blue"),
         lty = c(1, 2),
         lwd = 2,
         bty = "n")
}

#----------------------------------------------#
# Top 5 fraud types (2024–2025 focus)
#----------------------------------------------#
fraud_2024 <- fraud_daily %>%
  mutate(Date.Received...Date.recue = as.Date(Date.Received...Date.recue)) %>%
  filter(Date.Received...Date.recue >= as.Date("2024-01-01"))

top5fraud <- head(sort(table(fraud_2024$Fraud_type), decreasing = TRUE), 5)
top5_names <- names(top5fraud)

fraud_top5 <- fraud_daily %>%
  filter(Fraud_type %in% top5_names)

top5_list <- split(fraud_top5, fraud_top5$Fraud_type)

#----------------------------------------------#
# Plot: Top 5 fraud types
#----------------------------------------------#

par(mfrow = c(3, 3)) #Plotting all reports and top 5 fraud type reports together.
fig_num=2
for (ft in names(top5_list)) {
  
  df_ft <- top5_list[[ft]]
  
  plot(df_ft$Date.Received...Date.recue, #plotting 2021 to 2025 report count of top 5 fraud types.
       df_ft$total_victims,
       type = "l",
       main = ft,
       xlab = "Date",
       ylab = "Daily Victims")
  lines(smooth.spline(
    as.numeric(df_ft$Date.Received...Date.recue),
    df_ft$total_victims
  ), col = "blue", lwd = 2)
  
 label_ft <- if (grepl("fraud", ft, ignore.case = TRUE)) { #conditionally adding the word fraud if not in the name
  ft
} else {
  paste(ft, "Fraud")
}

mtext(paste("Fig", fig_num, ".", label_ft, "Reported Victims"), #captions
      side = 1, line = 4, cex = 0.6)
  fig_num <- fig_num + 1
}

#----------------------------------------------#
# Plot: All fraud (again for comparison)
#----------------------------------------------#

plot(fraud_all_daily$Date.Received...Date.recue,
     fraud_all_daily$total_victims,
     type = "l",
     xlab = "Date",
     ylab = "Total Daily Fraud Victims",
     main = "All Fraud Cases Combined (Daily)")
lines(smooth.spline(
    as.numeric(fraud_all_daily$Date.Received...Date.recue),
    fraud_all_daily$total_victims
  ), col = "blue", lwd = 2)
mtext(paste("Fig", 7, ".", "All Fraud Reported Victims"),
        side = 1, line = 4, cex = 0.6)


```

```{r Repeating stationary check for monetary loss}
# ------------------------------------------------------------#
# Aggregate Data: Total Monetary Loss                         #
# ------------------------------------------------------------#
fraud_all_daily.loss <- fraud_daily %>%
  mutate(Date.Received...Date.recue =
           as.Date(Date.Received...Date.recue)) %>%
  group_by(Date.Received...Date.recue) %>%
  summarise(total_loss = sum(total_loss, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(Date.Received...Date.recue)

# -----------------------------------------------------#
# Stationarity check: monetary loss                    #
# -----------------------------------------------------#
par(mfrow = c(3, 3))

for (ft in names(top5_list)) { #monetary loss of top 5 fraud types
  
  df_ft <- top5_list[[ft]]
  
  plot(df_ft$Date.Received...Date.recue,
       df_ft$total_loss,
       type = "l",
       main = ft,
       xlab = "Date",
       ylab = "Daily loss")
   lines(smooth.spline(
    as.numeric(df_ft$Date.Received...Date.recue),
    df_ft$total_loss
  ), col = "blue", lwd = 2)
  
 label_ft <- if (grepl("fraud", ft, ignore.case = TRUE)) { #conditionally adding the word fraud if not in the name
  ft
} else {
  paste(ft, "Fraud")
}

mtext(paste("Fig", fig_num, ".", label_ft, "Monetary Loss"), #captions
      side = 1, line = 4, cex = 0.6)
  fig_num <- fig_num + 1
}

plot(fraud_all_daily.loss$Date.Received...Date.recue, #monetary loss (total)
     fraud_all_daily.loss$total_loss,
     type = "l",
     xlab = "Date",
     ylab = "Total Daily Monetary Loss (CAD)",
     main = "All Fraud Cases Combined (Daily)")
lines(smooth.spline(
    as.numeric(fraud_all_daily.loss$Date.Received...Date.recue),
    fraud_all_daily.loss$total_loss
  ), col = "blue", lwd = 2)
mtext(paste("Fig", 13, ".", "All Reported Fraud Monetary Loss"),
        side = 1, line = 4, cex = 0.6)


# Note: relatively stationary with occasional spikes
```


```{r data split for top 5 fraud}
# ------------------------------------------------------------#
# Select top 5 fraud types (based on 2024 frequency)          #
# ------------------------------------------------------------#
# Skipping low-frequency fraud types due to insufficient data
top5fraud  <- head(sort(table(fraud_2024$Fraud_type), decreasing = TRUE), 5)
top5_names <- names(top5fraud)


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Filter dataset to top 5 fraud types           #
# ----------------------------------------------#
fraud_data_top5 <- fraud_daily %>%
  filter(Fraud_type %in% top5_names)


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Sort data by fraud type and date              #
# ----------------------------------------------#
fraud_data_top5 <- fraud_data_top5 %>%
  arrange(Fraud_type, Date.Received...Date.recue)


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Split into training and testing datasets      #
# ----------------------------------------------#
train_data <- fraud_data_top5 %>%
  filter(Date.Received...Date.recue < as.Date("2025-01-01"))

test_data <- fraud_data_top5 %>%
  filter(Date.Received...Date.recue >= as.Date("2025-01-01"))


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Split into lists by fraud type                #
# ----------------------------------------------#
train_list <- split(train_data, train_data$Fraud_type)
test_list  <- split(test_data,  test_data$Fraud_type)

```

```{r ARIMA model for total aggregation fraud count}
# ------------------------------------------------------------#
# ALL DATA: Train/Test Split                                  #
# ------------------------------------------------------------#
fraud_split_training <- fraud_all_daily %>%
  filter(Date.Received...Date.recue < as.Date("2025-01-01"))

fraud_split_testing <- fraud_all_daily %>%
  filter(Date.Received...Date.recue >= as.Date("2025-01-01"))

dates <- fraud_split_training$Date.Received...Date.recue  # store dates

fraud_total_train <- fraud_split_training[["total_victims"]]
fraud_total_test  <- fraud_split_testing[["total_victims"]]


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Convert data to Time Series                   #
# ----------------------------------------------#
fraud_total_train_ts <- ts(fraud_total_train, start = c(2021, 1), frequency = 7)
fraud_total_test_ts  <- ts(fraud_total_test,  start = c(2025, 1), frequency = 7)


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Fit ARIMA Model                               #
# ----------------------------------------------#
total.model <- auto.arima(fraud_total_train_ts, seasonal = TRUE)
res <- residuals(total.model)

# Residual diagnostics
tsdiag(total.model)
acf(res,  main = paste("Residual ACF -", ft))
pacf(res, main = paste("Residual PACF -", ft))


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Forecasting                                   #
# ----------------------------------------------#
fc.total <- forecast(total.model, h = 360)

train_dates  <- fraud_split_training$Date.Received...Date.recue
future_dates <- seq(from = max(train_dates) + 1,
                    by = "day",
                    length.out = 360)

plot(fc.total, include = 365)


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Rolling Window Validation                     #
# ----------------------------------------------#
window_size <- 30
n <- length(fraud_total_test_ts)
pred <- rep(NA, n)

fraud_total.rolling <- rolling_validate_arima(
  fraud_total_test_ts,
  fitted_model = total.model
)

plot(fraud_total.rolling$actual,
     type = "l",
     col = "red",
     lwd = 2,
     main = "Rolling Validation - Total Reports",
     ylab = "Cases",
     xlab = "Time")

lines(fraud_total.rolling$forecast,
      col = "blue",
      lwd = 2)

legend("topleft",
       legend = c("Actual", "Rolling Forecast"),
       col = c("red", "blue"),
       lty = 1,
       lwd = 2)

MAE  <- fraud_total.rolling$MAE
RMSE <- fraud_total.rolling$RMSE

actual_total_test <- as.numeric(fraud_total_test_ts)
range_test_total_test  <- max(actual_total_test ) - min(actual_total_test )
NMAE.rolling.total <- MAE/ range_test_total_test
NRMSE.rolling.total <- RMSE / range_test_total_test

# ----------------------------------------------#
# Naive Model For Comparsion                    #
# ----------------------------------------------#
naive_model <- naive(fraud_total_train_ts, h = length(fraud_total_test_ts))
actual.naive_total_test <- as.numeric(fraud_total_test_ts)
forecast.naive_total_test <- as.numeric(naive_model$mean)

#Normalizing MAE and RMSE
mae.naive_total_test  <- mean(abs(forecast.naive_total_test  - actual_total_test ))
rmse.naive_total_test <- sqrt(mean((forecast.naive_total_test  - actual_total_test )^2))



nmae.naive_total_test   <- mae.naive_total_test  / range_test_total_test 
nrmse.naive_total_test  <- rmse.naive_total_test  / range_test_total_test 

# ----------------------------------------------#
# Anomaly Injection                             #
# ----------------------------------------------#
inj <- inject_anomalies(fraud_total_test_ts)

fraud_total_test.contaminated <- inj$contaminated_series
spike_idx <- inj$spike_indices


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Fit model on 2024 data                        #
# ----------------------------------------------#
fraud_total.2024 <- fraud_all_daily %>%
  filter(Date.Received...Date.recue >= as.Date("2024-01-01"),
         Date.Received...Date.recue <  as.Date("2025-01-01"))

fraud_total_test.2024 <- fraud_total.2024[["total_victims"]]

fraud_total_2024.ts <- ts(
  fraud_total_test.2024,
  start = c(2024, 1),
  frequency = 7
)

ft_total.2024 <- Arima(fraud_total_2024.ts, model = total.model)


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Forecast over FULL horizon                    #
# ----------------------------------------------#
h <- length(fraud_total_test.contaminated)

fc.total.spike <- forecast(
  ft_total.2024,
  h = h,
  level = 95
)


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Anomaly detection determination               #
# ----------------------------------------------#
y_obs <- as.numeric(fraud_total_test.contaminated)

upper <- as.numeric(fc.total.spike$upper[, 1])

anomaly_flag <- y_obs > upper

detected_idx <- which(anomaly_flag)


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Plot: Forecast + Injected + Detected          #
# ----------------------------------------------#
correctly_detected <- intersect(spike_idx, detected_idx)

x <- seq_along(y_obs)

plot(x, y_obs,
     type = "l",
     col = "blue",
     xlab = "Test period index",
     ylab = "Value",
     main = "Forecast with Injected and Detected Anomalies")

lines(x, as.numeric(fc.total.spike$mean),  lty = 2)
lines(x, upper, lty = 3)
lines(x, as.numeric(fc.total.spike$lower[,1]), lty = 3)

points(spike_idx, y_obs[spike_idx],
       col = "red", pch = 16, cex = 1.2)

points(correctly_detected, y_obs[correctly_detected],
       col = "black", pch = 8, cex = 1.8, lwd = 2)

legend("topright",
       legend = c("Injected spikes", "Correctly detected"),
       col = c("red", "black"),
       pch = c(16, 8),
       pt.cex = c(1.2, 1.8),
       bty = "n")


# -----------------------------------------------------------#
# Recall over cumulative forecast window horizons            #
# -----------------------------------------------------------#

# Creating labels
n <- length(y_obs)

true_labels <- rep(0, n)
pred_labels <- rep(0, n)

true_labels[spike_idx] <- 1
pred_labels[detected_idx] <- 1


# Define cumulative horizons (e.g. every 30 days)
cutoffs <- seq(30, n, by = 30)

recall_values <- c()
precision_values <- c()
f1_values <- c()

for (c in cutoffs) {
  
  t <- true_labels[1:c]
  p <- pred_labels[1:c]
  
  TP <- sum(t == 1 & p == 1)
  FP <- sum(t == 0 & p == 1)
  FN <- sum(t == 1 & p == 0)
  TN <- sum(t == 0 & p == 0)
  
  #Setting to 0 if it doesn't exist. I.e. there was no anomalies to detect
  recall <- ifelse((TP + FN) == 0, 0, TP / (TP + FN))
  precision <- ifelse((TP + FP) == 0, 0, TP / (TP + FP))
  f1 <- ifelse((precision + recall) == 0, 0, 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall))
   
  recall_values <- c(recall_values, recall)
  precision_values <- c(precision_values, precision)
  f1_values        <- c(f1_values, f1)
}


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Plot: Recall vs Forecast Horizon              #
# ----------------------------------------------#

plot(cutoffs, recall_values,
     type = "b",
     col = "blue",
     pch = 16,
     ylim = c(0,1),
     xlab = "Forecast Horizon (Days)",
     ylab = "Recall",
     main = "Recall vs Forecast Horizon (Interval Detection)")

AD_total_df <- data.frame( #saving values for dashboard
  Fraud_Type = "Total",
  Horizon = cutoffs,
  Recall = recall_values,
  Precision = precision_values,
  F1 = f1_values
)
```

```{r Not useable ROC Curve}
# -----------------------------------------------------------#
# Loop: Injection, Detection and ROC Curve Creation (Failed) #
# -----------------------------------------------------------#
#NOTE: ROC curve was found to not be a useful metric given high specificity as a result of the synthetic anomaly injection using the standard data+(3*standard deviation), as well as the low alpha level of a 95% prediction interval. This part may be skipped.

# Defining the thresholds/prediction interval levels for ROC testing
levels_to_test <- c(seq(75, 95, by = 5), 97.5, 99, 99.5, 99.9)
roc_results <- data.frame(Level = numeric(), TPR = numeric(), FPR = numeric())

# Run the detection simulation
inj <- inject_anomalies(fraud_total_test.2024,k=3)
fraud_contaminated <- as.numeric(inj$contaminated_series)
spike_idx <- inj$spike_indices

for (lvl in levels_to_test) {
  
  # Generate forecast at the given confidence level
  fc_test <- forecast(ft_total.2024, h = length(fraud_contaminated), level = lvl)
  
  # Anomaly Detection for current level
  upper_bound <- as.numeric(fc_test$upper[, 1])
  detected_idx <- which(fraud_contaminated > upper_bound)
  
  # Confusion Matrix Calculations
  TP <- length(intersect(spike_idx, detected_idx))
  FP <- length(setdiff(detected_idx, spike_idx))
  FN <- length(setdiff(spike_idx, detected_idx))
  TN <- length(fraud_contaminated) - TP - FP - FN
  
  # Calculating Rates for ROC Curve
  TPR <- TP / (TP + FN) # Sensitivity
  FPR <- FP / (FP + TN) # 1 - Specificity
  
  roc_results <- rbind(roc_results, data.frame(Level = lvl, TPR = TPR, FPR = FPR))
}

# Plotting the Manual ROC Curve
plot(roc_results$FPR, roc_results$TPR, 
     type = "b", pch = 19, col = "blue",
     xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1),
     xlab = "False Positive Rate (1 - Specificity)",
     ylab = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)",
     main = "ROC Curve: Varying Forecast Intervals")

# 45 degree line to compare against random testing
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "red")

# Labeling the points with the confidence level
text(roc_results$FPR, roc_results$TPR, labels = roc_results$Level, pos = 4, cex = 0.7)


#As noted from above, due to the incredibly low false positive rate (FPR), the ROC is not a good measurement for the anomaly detection accuracy. Instead, it will be simpler and more useful to utilise the sensitivity as a metric.

```

```{r final model refit for total fraud}
# ----------------------------------------------#
# Final Model Refit (2023 onward)               #
# ----------------------------------------------#
fraud_full_refit <- fraud_all_daily %>%
  filter(Date.Received...Date.recue >= as.Date("2023-01-01"))

fraud_refit_2023 <- fraud_full_refit[["total_victims"]]

fraud_total_ts <- ts(fraud_refit_2023)

total.Refit <- Arima(fraud_total_ts, model = total.model)

fc.total.final <- forecast(total.Refit, h = 240)

plot(fc.total.final, include = 180)


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Export Forecast Table                         #
# ----------------------------------------------#
all_fraud_fc <- forecast_table(
  fc.total.final,
  "Total",
  "Victim_Count"
)


```

```{r TBATS model for total aggregation Monetary Loss}
# ----------------------------------------------#
# ALL DATA: Monetary Loss                       #
# ----------------------------------------------#
# TBATS exponential smoothing model
fraud_daily_training.loss <- fraud_all_daily.loss %>%
  filter(Date.Received...Date.recue < as.Date("2025-01-01"))

fraud_daily_testing.loss <- fraud_all_daily.loss %>%
  filter(Date.Received...Date.recue >= as.Date("2025-01-01"))


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Split into Training and Testing Data          #
# ----------------------------------------------#
daily_total_train.loss <- fraud_daily_training.loss[["total_loss"]]
daily_total_test.loss  <- fraud_daily_testing.loss[["total_loss"]]


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Convert to msts Objects                       #
# ----------------------------------------------#
fraud_train.loss.ts <- ts(daily_total_train.loss,7)
fraud_test.loss.ts  <- ts(daily_total_test.loss,7)

fraud_total_train.loss.ts <- msts(daily_total_train.loss, seasonal.periods = 7)
fraud_total_test.loss.ts  <- msts(daily_total_test.loss, seasonal.periods = 7)


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Fit Monetary Loss ARIMA Models                #
# ----------------------------------------------#
fit_arima.loss <- auto.arima(fraud_train.loss.ts)
fit_tbats <- tbats(fraud_total_train.loss.ts)

# ----------------------------------------------#
# Model Diagnostics                             #
# ----------------------------------------------#
summary(fit_tbats)
checkresiduals(fit_tbats)


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Forecast on Test Horizon                      #
# ----------------------------------------------#

fc_arima.loss <- forecast(fit_arima.loss,h=length(fraud_test.loss.ts))
fc_tbats <- forecast(fit_tbats, h = length(fraud_total_test.loss.ts))

fraud_total_test.loss.ts <- msts( #Aligning test data axis
  daily_total_test.loss,
  start = tsp(fc_tbats$mean)[1],
  seasonal.periods = 7
)

fraud_test.loss.ts <- ts( #Setting start index of test data to forecast
  fraud_test.loss.ts,
  start = start(fc_arima.loss$mean),
  frequency = frequency(fc_arima.loss$mean)
)
# ----------------------------------------------#
# Plot: Forecast vs Actual                      #
# ----------------------------------------------#
plot(fc_tbats, include = 30)

lines(fraud_total_test.loss.ts, col = "red")

legend("topleft",
       legend = c("actual", "forecast"),
       col = c("red", "blue"),
       pch = c(16, 16),
       pt.cex = c(1.2, 1.2),
       bty = "n")

accuracy(fc_tbats, fraud_total_test.loss.ts)

accuracy(fc_arima.loss,fraud_test.loss.ts)

# ----------------------------------------------#
# Naive Model For Comparsion                    #
# ----------------------------------------------#
actual_total_test.loss <- as.numeric(fraud_total_test.loss.ts)
range_test_total_test.loss  <- max(actual_total_test.loss ) - min(actual_total_test.loss )

naive_model.loss <- naive(fraud_train.loss.ts, h = length(fraud_total_test.loss.ts))
actual.naive_total_test.loss <- as.numeric(fraud_total_test.loss.ts)
forecast.naive_total_test.loss <- as.numeric(naive_model.loss$mean)

#Normalizing MAE and RMSE
mae.naive_total_test.loss  <- mean(abs(forecast.naive_total_test.loss  - actual_total_test.loss ))
rmse.naive_total_test.loss <- sqrt(mean((forecast.naive_total_test.loss  - actual_total_test.loss )^2))


nmae.naive_total_test.loss   <- mae.naive_total_test.loss  / range_test_total_test.loss 
nrmse.naive_total_test.loss  <- rmse.naive_total_test.loss  / range_test_total_test.loss 



# ----------------------------------------------#
# Final Refit on All Data                       #
# ----------------------------------------------#
all_daily.loss <- fraud_all_daily.loss[["total_loss"]]
all_daily.loss.tbat <- msts(all_daily.loss, seasonal.periods = 7)

fit_all_tbats.loss <- tbats(all_daily.loss.tbat)


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Final Forecasts                               #
# ----------------------------------------------#
fc_tbats_2026.loss <- forecast(fit_all_tbats.loss, h = 180)
plot(fc_tbats_2026.loss, include = 120)

fc_tbats_final_total.loss <- forecast(fit_all_tbats.loss, h = 240)
plot(fc_tbats_final_total.loss, include = 60)


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Convert Forecasts to Table                    #
# ----------------------------------------------#
all_Loss_fc <- forecast_table(
  fc_tbats_final_total.loss,
  "Total",
  "Monetary_Loss",
)
```

```{r ARIMA,TBATS, Naive Model NMAE and NRMSE}
# --------------------------------------------------------#
# Table for MAE and RMSE of ARIMA vs TBATS vs NAIVE       #
# --------------------------------------------------------#
library(kableExtra)

df <- data.frame(
  Model = c("ARIMA", "Naive", "TBATS","ARIMA", "Naive"),
  Outcome = c("Fraud Reports", "Fraud Reports", "Monetary Loss","Monetary Loss", "Monetary Loss"),
  `Train Size`=c(1458,1458,1458,1458,1458),
  `Test Size` = c(272, 272,272,272,272),
  NMAE = c(0.103, 0.228, 0.069,0.095,0.116),
  NRMSE = c(0.139, 0.266, 0.139,0.148,0.179),
  check.names = FALSE
)

tbl <- kbl(
  df,
  format = "html",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  align = c("l", "l", "c", "c", "c"),
  digits = 3
) |>
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    position = "center",
    font_size = 14
  ) |>
  column_spec(1, width = "4em") |>
  column_spec(2, width = "7em") |>
  column_spec(3, width = "4em") |>
  column_spec(4, width = "4em") |>
  column_spec(5, width = "4em")

tbl
```



```{r arima models top 5 fraud types}
# ---------------------------------------------------------#
# Fit ARIMA Models and Forecast Case Counts by Fraud Type  #
# ---------------------------------------------------------#
#Storing models, forecasts, and actual test cases
models <- list()
forecasts_cases <- list()
actual_cases <- list()
naive_forecasts_cases <- list()

#Creating an arima and naive model for each fraud type
for (ft in names(train_list)) {
  
  train_ft <- train_list[[ft]] #Extracting individual fraud type data from the matrix of training data
  test_ft  <- test_list[[ft]] #Extracting individual fraud type data from the matrix of testing data
  
  ts_cases <- ts( #Converting training data to a time series object for ARIMA modeling
    train_ft$total_victims,
    start = c(2021, 1),
    frequency = 7
  )
  
  # ARIMA Model        
  # -------------------#
  fit_cases <- auto.arima(ts_cases)
  h_cases <- nrow(test_ft)
  fc_cases <- forecast(fit_cases, h = h_cases)
  
  # Naive Model        
  # -------------------#
  fc_naive <- naive(ts_cases, h = h_cases)
  
  # Convert test set to time series object for forecasting against actual values later.
  test_cases_ts <- ts(
    test_ft$total_victims,
    start = time(fc_cases$mean)[1],
    frequency = frequency(fc_cases$mean)
  )
  
  #Storing models, forecasts, and actual data for each fraud type in the loop
  models[[ft]]               <- fit_cases
  forecasts_cases[[ft]]      <- fc_cases
  naive_forecasts_cases[[ft]] <- fc_naive   
  actual_cases[[ft]]         <- test_cases_ts
}


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Plot Forecast vs Actual for Each Fraud Type   #
# ----------------------------------------------#
for (ft in names(forecasts_cases)) {
  
  plot(forecasts_cases[[ft]], #Plotting forecast values
       main = paste("Forecast vs Actual -", ft),
       include = 60)
  
  lines(actual_cases[[ft]], col = "red", lwd = 2) #Overlaying actual values
  lines(naive_forecasts_cases[[ft]]$mean, col = "green", lwd = 2) #overlaying naive model forecast
  
  legend("topleft",
         legend = c("ARIMA", "Actual", "Naive"),
         col = c("blue", "red", "green"),
         lty = 1,
         lwd = 2)
}

# ----------------------------------------------#
# Rolling Window Validation                     #
# ----------------------------------------------#
rolling_results_cases <- list() #storing results of rolling window validation

for (ft in names(models)) {
  
  test_ft <- test_list[[ft]] #extracting actual value for validation
  
  test_cases_ts <- ts( #converting to time series object
    test_ft$total_victims,
    frequency = 7
  )
  
  rolling_results_cases[[ft]] <- rolling_validate_arima( #using rolling window function to return MAE,NMAE, RMSE, NRMSE
    series = test_cases_ts,
    fitted_model = models[[ft]],
    window_size = 14,
    h = 1
  )
}


# ----------------------------------------------#
# Plot Rolling Forecast vs Actual               #
# ----------------------------------------------#
for (ft in names(rolling_results_cases)) {
  
  res <- rolling_results_cases[[ft]]
  
  plot(res$actual,
       type = "l",
       col = "red",
       lwd = 2,
       main = paste("Rolling Validation -", ft),
       ylab = "Cases",
       xlab = "Time")
  
  lines(res$forecast, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
  
  legend("topleft",
         legend = c("Actual", "Rolling Forecast"),
         col = c("red", "blue"),
         lty = 1,
         lwd = 2)
}


# -------------------------------------------------------#
# Error Metrics Table Rolling Window Cross Validation    #
# -------------------------------------------------------#

results_table_cases <- data.frame( #storing results of rolling window cross validation
  Fraud_Type = names(rolling_results_cases),
  Model = "ARIMA",
  NMAE = sapply(rolling_results_cases, function(x) { #computing normalized MAE
    valid_actual <- x$actual[!is.na(x$forecast)]
    x$MAE / (max(valid_actual) - min(valid_actual))
  }),
  
  NRMSE = sapply(rolling_results_cases, function(x) {#computing normalized RMSE
    valid_actual <- x$actual[!is.na(x$forecast)]
    x$RMSE / (max(valid_actual) - min(valid_actual))
  })
)
# ----------------------------------------------#
# One Time Validation                           #
# ----------------------------------------------#

#Validation metrics NRMSE, NMAE for each fraud type; one-time fitting ARIMA and Naive Model
for (fraud_type in names(test_list)) {
  
  # Extract test data
  test_data <- test_list[[fraud_type]]
  
  #convert to time series object for forecasting
  test_ts <- ts(
    test_data$total_victims,
    frequency = 7
  )
  
  h <- length(test_ts)
  

  # ARIMA Forecast     
  # -------------------#
  fc_arima <- forecasts_cases[[fraud_type]]
  pred_arima <- as.numeric(fc_arima$mean)
  

  # Naive Forecast     
  # -------------------#
  # Fitting naive model + forecast
  fc_naive <- naive_forecasts_cases[[fraud_type]] #extracting naive model forecasts from earlier for each fraud type

  pred_naive <- as.numeric(fc_naive$mean) #converting to numeric for comparison against actuals

  # Actual data      
  # -------------------#
  actual <- as.numeric(test_ts) #Converting actual data to numeric for comparison

  # ARIMA Metrics      
  # -------------------#
  res_arima <- actual - pred_arima #Computing residuals
  
  MAE_arima  <- mean(abs(res_arima)) #Computing Mean Absolute Error
  RMSE_arima <- sqrt(mean(res_arima^2)) #Computing Root Mean Square Error
  

  # Naive Metrics      
  # -------------------#
  res_naive <- actual - pred_naive #Computing residuals
  
  MAE_naive  <- mean(abs(res_naive)) #Computing Mean Absolute Error
  RMSE_naive <- sqrt(mean(res_naive^2)) #Computing Root Mean Square Error
  
  # Normalization     
  # -------------------#
  range_val <- max(actual) - min(actual) #Computing Range of actual test data for normalization
  

  # Appending BOTH        
  # -------------------#
  
# Append ARIMA results
 results_table_cases <- rbind(
  results_table_cases,
  data.frame(
    Fraud_Type = paste0(fraud_type, " ", "(One-Time Validation)"),
    Model = "ARIMA",
    NMAE = MAE_arima / range_val,
    NRMSE = RMSE_arima / range_val
  )
)

# Append NAIVE results 
results_table_cases <- rbind(
  results_table_cases,
  data.frame(
    Fraud_Type = paste0(fraud_type),
    Model = "NAIVE",
    NMAE = MAE_naive / range_val,
    NRMSE = RMSE_naive / range_val
  )
)
}

#Reordering to keep ARIMA results together and NAIVE results together.
results_table_cases <- results_table_cases[
  order(results_table_cases$Model, results_table_cases$Fraud_Type),
]

#Using Kable for reformatting of table for easier viewing.
top5_fraud_tbl<- kbl(
  results_table_cases,
  format = "html",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  align = c("l", "l", "c", "c"),
  digits = 3,
  row.names=FALSE
) |>
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    position = "center",
    font_size = 14
  ) |>
  column_spec(1, width = "4em") |>
  column_spec(2, width = "7em") |>
  column_spec(3, width = "4em") |>
  column_spec(4, width = "4em") 

top5_fraud_tbl
```

```{r anomaly detection of top 5 fraud type models}
#--------------------------------------#
# Top 5 Anomaly Detection validation   #
#--------------------------------------#

#List objects to store anomaly data
anomaly_data <- list()
forecast_anomaly <- list()
actual_anomaly   <- list()
detected <- list()
confusion_matrix.top5 <- list()

#For each fraud type, inject synthetic anomalies into the test data with the inject_anomalies function defined earlier.
for (ft in names(test_list)) {
  
  test_series <- test_list[[ft]]$total_victims
  
  injected <- inject_anomalies(test_series, k = 3)
  
  anomaly_data[[ft]] <- list(
    contaminated = injected$contaminated_series,
    true_idx     = injected$spike_indices
  )
}

#For each fraud type, 
for (ft in names(models)) {
  
  contaminated_ts <- ts(
    anomaly_data[[ft]]$contaminated,
    frequency = 7
  )
  
  h <- length(contaminated_ts)
  
  fc <- forecast(models[[ft]], h = h)
  
  forecast_anomaly[[ft]] <- as.numeric(fc$upper[, "95%"])
  actual_anomaly[[ft]]   <- as.numeric(contaminated_ts)
}

for (ft in names(forecast_anomaly)) {
  
  predicted<- forecast_anomaly[[ft]]
  actual <- actual_anomaly[[ft]]

  
  detected_idx <- which(actual > predicted)
  
  detected[[ft]] <- detected_idx
}

#------------------------------------------#
# Creating Confusion Matrix and Results    #
#------------------------------------------#

for (ft in names(detected)) {
  
  true_idx <- anomaly_data[[ft]]$true_idx
  detected_idx <- detected[[ft]]
  
  n <- length(actual_anomaly[[ft]])
  
  #Result
  true_labels <- rep(0, n)
  predicted_labels <- rep(0, n)
  
  true_labels[true_idx] <- 1
  predicted_labels[detected_idx] <- 1
  
  # Confusion matrix 
  TP <- sum(true_labels == 1 & predicted_labels == 1)
  FP <- sum(true_labels == 0 & predicted_labels == 1)
  TN <- sum(true_labels == 0 & predicted_labels == 0)
  FN <- sum(true_labels == 1 & predicted_labels == 0)
  
  confusion_matrix.top5[[ft]] <- data.frame(
    Fraud_Type = ft,
    TP = TP,
    FP = FP,
    TN = TN,
    FN = FN,
    Precision = TP / (TP + FP),
    Recall    = TP / (TP + FN),
    F1        = 2 * TP / (2 * TP + FP + FN)
  )
}
confusion_table <- do.call(rbind, confusion_matrix.top5)
print(confusion_table)

#--------------------------------------#
# Plotting anomaly detection           #
#--------------------------------------#
for (ft in names(detected)) {
  
  x <- seq_along(actual_anomaly[[ft]])
  y_obs <- actual_anomaly[[ft]]
  
  spike_idx <- anomaly_data[[ft]]$true_idx
  detected_idx <- detected[[ft]]
  
  correctly_detected <- intersect(spike_idx, detected_idx)
  false_detected <- setdiff(detected_idx, spike_idx)
  
  plot(x, y_obs,
       type = "l",
       col = "blue",
       xlab = "Test period index",
       ylab = "Value",
       main = paste("Forecast with Injected and Detected Anomalies -", ft))
  
  lines(x, as.numeric(forecast_anomaly[[ft]]), lty = 2)
  
  points(spike_idx, y_obs[spike_idx],
         col = "red", pch = 16, cex = 1.2)
  
  points(correctly_detected, y_obs[correctly_detected],
         col = "black", pch = 8, cex = 1.8, lwd = 2)
  
  points(false_detected, y_obs[false_detected],
         col = "purple", pch = 4, cex = 1.5, lwd = 2)
  
  legend("topright",
         legend = c("Injected spikes", "Correctly detected", "False positives"),
         col = c("red", "black", "purple"),
         pch = c(16, 8, 4),
         pt.cex = c(1.2, 1.8, 1.5),
         bty = "n")
}

#---------------------------------------------------------------#
# Anomaly Detection validation by number of months forecasted   #
#---------------------------------------------------------------#
n_runs <- 10
all_cumulative_runs <- list()

for (run in 1:n_runs) {
  
  anomaly_data <- list()
  forecast_anomaly <- list()
  actual_anomaly   <- list()
  detected <- list()
  
  # -----------------------#
  # Inject anomalies       #
  # -----------------------#
  for (ft in names(test_list)) {
    
    test_series <- test_list[[ft]]$total_victims
    
    injected <- inject_anomalies(test_series, k = 3)
    
    anomaly_data[[ft]] <- list(
      contaminated = injected$contaminated_series,
      true_idx     = injected$spike_indices
    )
  }
  

  # Forecast          
  # ------------------#
  for (ft in names(models)) {
    
    contaminated_ts <- ts(anomaly_data[[ft]]$contaminated, frequency = 7)
    h <- length(contaminated_ts)
    
    fc <- forecast(models[[ft]], h = h)
    
    forecast_anomaly[[ft]] <- as.numeric(fc$upper[,"95%"])
    actual_anomaly[[ft]]   <- as.numeric(contaminated_ts)
  }
  

  # Detection     
  # --------------#
  for (ft in names(forecast_anomaly)) {
    
    predicted <- forecast_anomaly[[ft]]
    actual <- actual_anomaly[[ft]]

    detected[[ft]] <- which(actual > predicted)
  }
  

  # Cumulative Evaluation Metrics 
  # ------------------------------#
  
  cumulative_results_all <- list()
  
  for (ft in names(models)) {
    
    y <- actual_anomaly[[ft]]
    n <- length(y)
    
    spike_idx <- anomaly_data[[ft]]$true_idx
    detected_idx <- detected[[ft]]
    
    true_labels <- rep(0, n)
    pred_labels <- rep(0, n)
    
    true_labels[spike_idx] <- 1
    pred_labels[detected_idx] <- 1
    
    cutoffs <- seq(30, n, by = 30)
    
    df <- data.frame()
    
    for (c in cutoffs) {
      
      t <- true_labels[1:c]
      p <- pred_labels[1:c]
      
      TP <- sum(t == 1 & p == 1)
      FP <- sum(t == 0 & p == 1)
      FN <- sum(t == 1 & p == 0)
      
      df <- rbind(df, data.frame(
        Fraud_Type = ft,
        Horizon = c,
        TP = TP,
        FP = FP,
        FN = FN,
        Run = run
      ))
    }
    
    cumulative_results_all[[ft]] <- df
  }
  
  all_cumulative_runs[[run]] <- do.call(rbind, cumulative_results_all)
}

#Pooling results for all 10 runs
all_cumulative_df <- do.call(rbind, all_cumulative_runs)

pooled_results <- aggregate(cbind(TP, FP, FN) ~ Fraud_Type + Horizon,
                            data = all_cumulative_df,
                            sum)

#Computing Metrics with pooled results
pooled_results$Precision <- with(pooled_results,
                                 ifelse((TP + FP) == 0, 0, TP / (TP + FP)))

pooled_results$Recall <- with(pooled_results,
                              ifelse((TP + FN) == 0, 0, TP / (TP + FN)))

pooled_results$F1 <- with(pooled_results,
                          ifelse((Precision + Recall) == 0, 0,
                                 2 * Precision * Recall / (Precision + Recall)))


cumulative_results <- pooled_results[, c("Fraud_Type", "Horizon", "Recall", "Precision", "F1")]
fraud_types <- unique(cumulative_results$Fraud_Type)


#Plotting F1-Score versus forecast horizon 95% intervals for all 5 fraud types
plot(NULL,
     xlim = c(0, max(cumulative_results$Horizon)),
     ylim = c(0, 1),
     xlab = "Forecast Horizon (Days)",
     ylab = "F1-Score",
     main = "Anomaly Detection vs Forecast Horizon")

colors <- rainbow(length(fraud_types))

for (i in seq_along(fraud_types)) {
  
  ft <- fraud_types[i]
  df <- subset(cumulative_results, Fraud_Type == ft)
  
  lines(df$Horizon,
        df$F1,
        type = "b",
        col = colors[i],
        pch = 16)
}

legend("bottomleft",
       legend = fraud_types,
       col = colors,
       lty = 1,
       pch = 16,
       cex = 0.8)

#Saving all anomaly detection metrics to a single csv and exporting for powerbi
AD_all_df <- rbind(AD_total_df, cumulative_results)
write.csv(AD_all_df,
          here("output", "recall_vs_horizon_all_models.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r refitting and final top 5 fraud report count models}
#--------------------------------------#
# Refitting models on full data now    #
#--------------------------------------#
full_data <- split(fraud_data_top5, fraud_data_top5$Fraud_type)

fc_final_ft <- list()
for (ft in names(full_data)) {
  
  # Get the full dataset for this fraud type
  df <- full_data[[ft]]
  
  #Change to time series
   ts_full <- ts(
    df$total_victims,
    start = c(2021, 1),  # adjust if your data doesn’t start in week 1 of 2021
    frequency = 7        # weekly frequency
  )
  old_arima <- arimaorder(models[[ft]])
fit_full.arima <- Arima(ts_full, order = old_arima[1:3], 
                        seasonal = old_arima[4:6])
  fc_final_ft[[ft]] <-forecast(fit_full.arima,h=240)
}

#--------------------------------------#
# Convert Forecasts to Table           #
#--------------------------------------#
all_forecasts <- do.call(
  rbind,
  lapply(names(fc_final_ft), function(ft) {
    
    fc <- fc_final_ft[[ft]]
    
    forecast_table(
      fc          = fc,
      fraud_type  = ft,
      metric      = "Victim_Count"
    )
  })
)
  
```


```{r modeling top 5 fraud types monetary loss}
#-----------------------------------------------------------#
# Fit ARIMA Models and Forecast monetary loss by Fraud Type #
#-----------------------------------------------------------#
models.loss <- list()
forecasts_cases.loss <- list()
actual_cases.loss <- list()
normalized_metrics <- list()

  for (ft in names(train_list)) {
  
  train_ft <- train_list[[ft]]
  test_ft  <- test_list[[ft]]
  
  # ------------------------#
  # Changing to time series #
  # ------------------------#
  ts_cases.loss <- ts(
    train_ft$total_loss,
    start = c(2021, 1),
    frequency = 7
  )
  
  # Create ARIMA model
  fit_cases.loss <- auto.arima(ts_cases.loss)
  
  h_cases <- nrow(test_ft)
  
  # Forecast the ARIMA model
  fc_cases.loss <- forecast(fit_cases.loss, h = h_cases)
  
  # Convert test cases to time series
  test_cases_ts.loss <- ts(
    test_ft$total_loss,
    start = time(fc_cases.loss$mean)[1],
    frequency = frequency(fc_cases.loss$mean)
  )
  
  models.loss[[ft]]          <- fit_cases.loss
  forecasts_cases.loss[[ft]] <- fc_cases.loss
  actual_cases.loss[[ft]]    <- test_cases_ts.loss
  
  #computing MAE and RMSE of
  acc <- accuracy(fc_cases.loss, test_cases_ts.loss)
  range_val <- max(test_cases_ts.loss) - min(test_cases_ts.loss)
   normalized_metrics[[ft]] <- data.frame(
    NMAE  = acc["Test set", "MAE"] / range_val,
    NRMSE = acc["Test set", "RMSE"] / range_val
  )
  }

#-----------------------------------------------------------#
# Fit TBATS Models and Forecast monetary loss by Fraud Type #
#-----------------------------------------------------------#

models_tbats.loss <- list()
forecasts_cases_tbats.loss <- list()
actual_cases_tbats.loss <- list()
normalized_metrics_tbats <- list()

 for (ft in names(train_list)) {
  train_ft <- train_list[[ft]]
  test_ft  <- test_list[[ft]]
# --------------------------#
# Changing to time series   #
# --------------------------#
  ts_cases.loss <- ts(
    train_ft$total_loss,
    start = c(2021, 1),
    frequency=7
  )
  
  # Create TBATS model
  fit_cases.loss <- tbats(ts_cases.loss)
  
  h_cases <- nrow(test_ft)
  
  # Forecast the ARIMA model
  fc_cases_tbats.loss <- forecast(fit_cases.loss, h = h_cases)
  
  # Convert test cases to time series
  test_cases_ts.loss <- ts(
    test_ft$total_loss,
    start = time(fc_cases_tbats.loss$mean)[1],
    frequency = frequency(fc_cases_tbats.loss$mean)
  )
  
  models_tbats.loss[[ft]]          <- fit_cases.loss
  forecasts_cases_tbats.loss[[ft]] <- fc_cases_tbats.loss
  actual_cases_tbats.loss[[ft]]    <- test_cases_ts.loss
  
  #computing MAE and RMSE of
  acc <- accuracy(fc_cases_tbats.loss, test_cases_ts.loss)
  range_val <- max(test_cases_ts.loss) - min(test_cases_ts.loss)
   normalized_metrics_tbats[[ft]] <- data.frame(
    NMAE  = acc["Test set", "MAE"] / range_val,
    NRMSE = acc["Test set", "RMSE"] / range_val
  )
  }

#-----------------------------------------------------------#
# Fit NAIVE Models and Forecast monetary loss by Fraud Type #
#-----------------------------------------------------------#
normalized_metrics_naive <- list()

for (ft in names(train_list)) {
  
  train_ft <- train_list[[ft]]
  test_ft  <- test_list[[ft]]
  
  ts_cases.loss <- ts(
    train_ft$total_loss,
    start = c(2021, 1),
    frequency = 7
  )
  
  h_cases <- nrow(test_ft)
  
  # -------------------#
  # Naive Model        #
  # -------------------#
  fc_naive.loss <- naive(ts_cases.loss, h = h_cases)
  
  test_cases_ts.loss <- ts(
    test_ft$total_loss,
    start = time(fc_naive.loss$mean)[1],
    frequency = frequency(fc_naive.loss$mean)
  )
  
  acc <- accuracy(fc_naive.loss, test_cases_ts.loss)
  range_val <- max(test_cases_ts.loss) - min(test_cases_ts.loss)
  
  normalized_metrics_naive[[ft]] <- data.frame(
    NMAE  = acc["Test set", "MAE"] / range_val,
    NRMSE = acc["Test set", "RMSE"] / range_val
  )
}

naive_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(normalized_metrics_naive), function(nm) {
  df <- normalized_metrics_naive[[nm]]
  df$Fraud_Type <- nm
  df
}))

#renaming columns
names(naive_df)[1:2] <- c("NMAE_NAIVE", "NRMSE_NAIVE")
# -----------------------#
# Combining results      #
# -----------------------#
arima_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(normalized_metrics), function(nm) { #converting to a single dataframe
  df <- normalized_metrics[[nm]]
  df$Fraud_Type <- nm
  df
}))

tbats_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(normalized_metrics_tbats), function(nm) { #converting to a single dataframe
  df <- normalized_metrics_tbats[[nm]]
  df$Fraud_Type <- nm
  df
}))

names(arima_df)[1:2] <- c("NMAE_ARIMA", "NRMSE_ARIMA")
names(tbats_df)[1:2] <- c("NMAE_TBATS", "NRMSE_TBATS")

combined_df.loss <- merge(arima_df, tbats_df, by = "Fraud_Type") #Combining arima and tbats dataframes
combined_df.loss <- merge(combined_df.loss, naive_df, by = "Fraud_Type") #combining with naive model results

top5_loss_tbl<- kbl(
  combined_df.loss,
  format = "html",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  align = c("l", "l", "c", "c","c","c","c"),
  digits = 3,
  row.names=FALSE
) |>
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    position = "center",
    font_size = 14
  ) |>
  column_spec(1, width = "4em") |>
  column_spec(2, width = "7em") |>
  column_spec(3, width = "4em") |>
  column_spec(4, width = "4em") |>
  column_spec(5, width = "4em") |>
  column_spec(6, width = "4em") |>
  column_spec(7, width = "4em") 

top5_loss_tbl

#--------------------------------------#
# Refitting models on full data now    #
#--------------------------------------#
full_data <- split(fraud_data_top5, fraud_data_top5$Fraud_type)

fc_final_ft_tbats.loss <- list()
final_tbats_model.loss <- list()
for (ft in names(full_data)) {
  
  # Get the full dataset for this fraud type
  df <- full_data[[ft]]
  
  #Change to time series
   ts_full <- ts(
    df$total_loss,
    start = c(2021, 1),
    frequency = 7      
  )
  final_tbats_model.loss[[ft]] <- tbats(ts_full,models_tbats.loss[[ft]])
  fc_final_ft_tbats.loss[[ft]] <- forecast(final_tbats_model.loss[[ft]],h=240)
}

#Removing Identity fraud as it has no values
fc_final_ft_tbats.loss <- fc_final_ft_tbats.loss[
  names(fc_final_ft_tbats.loss) != "Identity Fraud"
]

for (ft in names(fc_final_ft_tbats.loss)) {
  
  plot(fc_final_ft_tbats.loss[[ft]],
       main = paste("Forecast -", ft))

}

#--------------------------------------#
# Convert Forecasts to Table           #
#--------------------------------------#
all_forecasts_tbats.loss <- do.call(
  rbind,
  lapply(names(fc_final_ft_tbats.loss), function(ft) {
    fc <- fc_final_ft_tbats.loss[[ft]]
    forecast_table(fc = fc, fraud_type = ft, metric = "Monetary_Loss")
  })
)
```


```{r combining forecasts and exporting}
#--------------------------------------#
# Combining forecasts                  #
#--------------------------------------#
tables <- list(
  all_fraud_fc,
  all_forecasts,
  all_Loss_fc,
  all_forecasts_tbats.loss
)

#Converting all time series columns to numeric vectors
make_numeric_ts <- function(df) {
  df[] <- lapply(df, function(x) if (is.ts(x)) as.numeric(x) else x)
  df
}
tables <- lapply(tables, make_numeric_ts)
combined_table <- do.call(rbind, tables)
write.csv(combined_table, here("output", "combined_forecasts.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

```
